description:
## The change

Add four \`#[cfg(test)]\` thread-local counters to \`src/ir_typer.rs\`,
incremented at known sites:

  - WORKLIST_POPS    — \`process_worklist\` pop iterations
  - TYPE_FN_CALLS    — calls to \`type_fn\` from the worklist
  - WALK_CALLS       — invocations of \`walk_spec_for_discovery\`
  - (FINAL_SPEC_COUNT is just \`mt.specs.len()\` — no counter needed)

Precedent: \`TYPE_MODULE_CALLS\` (same file, line ~447). Zero
release-build cost — gated on \`#[cfg(test)]\`.

Add a test pinning generous upper bounds on each metric for both
ast_eval (the worst-case spec-explosion fixture) and fib_tailrec
(a baseline shape — recursive but flat). Pin bounds, not exact
values: improvements stay green, regressions fail with a message
identifying which axis regressed.

\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn ast_eval_typer_work_bounded() {
    let m = lower_fixture("ast_eval");
    reset_typer_counters();
    let mt = type_module(&m);
    let pops    = typer_counter(WORKLIST_POPS);
    let walks   = typer_counter(WALK_CALLS);
    let typefns = typer_counter(TYPE_FN_CALLS);
    let specs   = mt.specs.len();

    // Bounds are ~2× current observed values. Tighten on intentional
    // improvements. Never reflex-bless on regression — investigate.
    assert!(pops    <  500, "worklist pops regressed: {}",   pops);
    assert!(walks   <  500, "walk calls regressed: {}",      walks);
    assert!(typefns <  150, "type_fn calls regressed: {}",   typefns);
    assert!(specs   <  100, "final spec count regressed: {}", specs);
}

#[test]
fn fib_tailrec_typer_work_bounded() { /* same shape, tighter bounds */ }
\`\`\`

## Why counters, not wall-clock

Wall-clock is brittle: CI runner speed varies, runs flake under
load, generous margins catch only catastrophic regressions, tight
margins are forced to be loose for cross-machine portability.

Counters are deterministic: independent of CPU, load, OS scheduler.
A failure points at *which* axis regressed (worklist iterations vs.
type_fn count vs. final spec count), which is the diagnostic info
you actually want.

## When to bless

When PROV.6 lands and intentionally changes any counter (likely
\`worklist_pops\` drops further), tighten the bound in the same
commit. Never bless reflexively in response to a red test.

## Why now

This ticket lands BEFORE PROV.6 so the perf pin catches any
unintended regression from the refactor. The bounds reflect
current values; PROV.6 will tighten them when it lands.

## Acceptance

- Four counters added, zero release-build cost.
- Two tests pinning bounds for ast_eval + fib_tailrec.
- Tests pass on current code.

## Independent of

All other PROV tickets. Lands before PROV.6 by recommendation.
title: fz-PROV.4: pin deterministic typer-work counters for ast_eval + fib_tailrec
